Imports

../strategy, ../strategies/base, ../indicators, ../core, schema

Types

BuildError = object of CatchableError
Error during strategy building 
DeclarativeStrategy = ref object of Strategy
  strategyDef*: StrategyYAML
  indicators*: Table[string, IndicatorInstance]
  indicatorSources*: Table[string, string]
  indicatorOutputs*: Table[string, string]
  lastSignal*: Position
  previousValues*: Table[string, float64]
A strategy built from YAML definition 
IndicatorInstance = ref object
  case kind*: IndicatorKind
  of ikMA:
    ma*: MA
  of ikEMA:
    ema*: EMA
  of ikRSI:
    rsi*: RSI
  of ikMACD:
    macd*: MACD
  of ikBollinger:
    bb*: BollingerBands
  of ikSTOCH:
    stoch*: STOCH
  of ikCCI:
    cci*: CCI
  of ikMFI:
    mfi*: MFI
  of ikADX:
    adx*: ADX
  of ikATR:
    atr*: ATR
  of ikOBV:
    obv*: OBV
  of ikAROON:
    aroon*: AROON
  of ikPSAR:
    psar*: PSAR
  of ikTRIMA:
    trima*: TRIMA
  of ikDEMA:
    dema*: DEMA
  of ikTEMA:
    tema*: TEMA
  of ikKAMA:
    kama*: KAMA
  of ikMV:
    mv*: MV
  of ikSTDEV:
    stdev*: STDEV
  of ikTRANGE:
    trange*: TRANGE
  of ikNATR:
    natr*: NATR
  of ikAD:
    ad*: AD
  of ikMOM:
    mom*: MOM
  of ikCMO:
    cmo*: CMO
  of ikSTOCHRSI:
    stochrsi*: STOCHRSI
  of ikPPO:
    ppo*: PPO
Runtime indicator instance with type-erased interface 
IndicatorKind = enum
  ikMA, ikEMA, ikRSI, ikMACD, ikBollinger, ikSTOCH, ikCCI, ikMFI, ikADX, ikATR,
  ikOBV, ikAROON, ikPSAR, ikTRIMA, ikDEMA, ikTEMA, ikKAMA, ikMV, ikSTDEV,
  ikTRANGE, ikNATR, ikAD, ikMOM, ikCMO, ikSTOCHRSI, ikPPO
Supported indicator types

Procs

proc buildStrategy(strategyDef: StrategyYAML): DeclarativeStrategy {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, BuildError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Build an executable strategy from YAML definition Creates indicator instances and prepares for execution 
proc createIndicator(indicatorDef: IndicatorYAML): IndicatorInstance {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, BuildError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create an indicator instance from YAML definition This is the semi-automated factory approach for Phase 1 
proc evaluateCondition(s: DeclarativeStrategy; condition: ConditionYAML;
                       bar: OHLCV): bool {....raises: [KeyError, BuildError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Evaluate a condition (recursive for AND/OR) Returns true if condition is met 
proc getValue(ind: IndicatorInstance; subfield: string = ""): float64 {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get current value from indicator Subfield allows accessing sub-values like "signal" for MACD 
proc parseReference(s: DeclarativeStrategy; refStr: string; bar: OHLCV): float64 {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, BuildError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Parse a reference and return its value References can be:
  • Indicator IDs: "rsi_14"
  • Indicator subfields: "macd_12_26_9.signal"
  • Special keywords: "price", "volume", etc.
  • Literal numbers: "30", "70"
proc updateIndicator(ind: IndicatorInstance; bar: OHLCV;
                     source: string = "close") {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Update indicator with new bar source specifies which field to use: open, high, low, close, volume Note: Returns nothing, use getValue() to get current value

Methods

method getPositionSizing(s: DeclarativeStrategy): tuple[
    sizingType: PositionSizingType, value: float] {.
    ...raises: [BuildError, KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Return position sizing configuration from YAML definition 
method onBar(s: DeclarativeStrategy; bar: OHLCV): Signal {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, BuildError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Process a single bar and generate signal Updates all indicators and evaluates entry/exit rules 
method reset(s: DeclarativeStrategy) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Reset strategy state

Exports

PositionSizingType
